data.description:
## Why
Change #529's sole advisory — 'the new degrade behavior has no test; all classifier tests importorskip pydantic_ai so a core-only env never exercises it' — scored severity none / impact 0.0, then proved out as a red-main incident 57 minutes after merge (fix #530). No threshold can catch a finding scored 0.0: this is a pass-2/impact-model vocabulary gap in impact_code (src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py:230-267), whose maint lane has safety_net_removal_without_replacement but nothing for 'change introduces/unmasks a reachable path with NO automated coverage'. Distinct from the blocking-lane stories: those tune pass-3 thresholds; this fixes what pass 2 can express.

## What
- Add a maint-lane binary (e.g. reachable_path_without_automated_coverage, tier moderate) to the code-review verification model + the verify prompt (src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-verify.md), with abstain-safe default (missing -> 0, never inflates).
- Per ADR 0036 a formula/vocabulary change REQUIRES an IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump (code-v2 -> code-v3 in src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py) and a fresh version-segmented cohort before any threshold consumes it.
- Replay #529's finding through the extended model and record its new impact in a test fixture.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The new binary exists in the code-review verification model + verify prompt with an abstain-safe default and clear none/low/medium/high definitions.
- [ ] IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION is bumped to code-v3 and sidecars carry it.
- [ ] A unit test replays a #529-shaped finding and asserts impact > 0 under code-v3 while the code-v2 path is byte-unchanged for old sidecars.
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make typecheck passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.
data.title: impact_code lane gap: untested-degradation / unmasked-regression findings score 0.0 (code-v3 bump)
